title: Merck — White Space
html:
Merck — White-Space BD Map

Last updated: 2026-04-10 AO: Dave Johnson | AD: Melissa Weinstein FY25: $1.8M | FY26 Target: $2.1M (+20%) | FY26 YTD: $666K (32%) Gap to target: $766K | Weighted pipeline: $671K Vinoth's cluster: East 7 (Chanakya, Vidhya, Madhur)

1. Brand x Service Matrix

Service Keytruda (pan-tumor) HIV (doravirine/islatravir) Gardasil (HPV) RSV Vaccine EyeBio (ophth.) PCSK-9 (cardio) Pneumococcal (peds) Prevymis (transplant) Enflonsia
PET ACTIVE (NSCLC) ACTIVE Revisiting -- Opportunity Opportunity Had projects -- ACTIVE
ATU Opportunity -- -- -- Opportunity -- -- ACTIVE (Kidney) --
HCP-Patient Opportunity -- -- -- Opportunity -- -- -- --
MSL Tracking Opportunity -- -- -- -- -- -- -- --
Digital Tracking Opportunity -- -- -- -- Opportunity -- -- ACTIVE
Message Testing ACTIVE (Global) -- -- -- Opportunity Opportunity -- -- --
Qual Opportunity -- -- -- -- -- -- ACTIVE (KT Patient) --
Launch KPI N/A -- -- -- Opportunity Opportunity -- -- --
Global RT/Retainer -- ACTIVE ($125K) -- -- -- -- -- -- --
Social Listening Opportunity -- -- Opportunity -- -- -- -- --

Legend: ACTIVE = booked/in-flight | Opportunity = viable pitch | -- = low fit or no signal

2. Top 10 Opportunities (Ranked)

Priority tier: Renewals and expansions (protect the base)

# Opportunity Brand Est. Value Confidence Rationale
1 Keytruda NSCLC PET renewal + expansion Keytruda $150-200K High Must-renew. Explore adding new tumor types (HNSCC, melanoma) as line extensions to PET.
2 HIV retainer renewal + global expansion HIV $125-175K High First global win. Renew and pitch additional markets or additional waves. Retainer model = sticky revenue.
3 Enflonsia PET + Digital Tracker renewal Enflonsia $80-120K High Active and presumably performing. Standard renewal path.
4 Prevymis ATU/Qual renewal Prevymis $60-80K High Active in Kidney ATU + KT Patient Qual. Renewal is the first move.

Priority tier: Near-term new wins (close the gap)

# Opportunity Brand Est. Value Confidence Rationale
5 Gardasil PET reactivation Gardasil $80-120K Medium Already revisiting PET. If Merck is re-investing in Gardasil post-competitive pressure, this could close fast.
6 Keytruda Global Message Testing expansion Keytruda $100-150K Medium Currently ramping. Upsell additional markets or indications.
7 Keytruda ATU (new indication) Keytruda $80-120K Medium Keytruda dominates but faces biosimilar horizon (2028+). Brand team may want ATU data to defend share in specific tumors.
8 EyeBio launch services (PET + ATU + Launch KPI) EyeBio $100-200K Low-Medium New launch = new budget. But new division, new contacts needed. Requires Rhonda Harp to bridge intro.

Priority tier: Speculative (park unless signal emerges)

# Opportunity Brand Est. Value Confidence Rationale
9 PCSK-9 launch services PCSK-9 $80-150K Low Pipeline asset, launch timing uncertain. Worth a conversation with Rhonda to understand timeline.
10 Keytruda MSL Tracking Keytruda $60-80K Low Large MSL field force. But MSL Tracking is harder to sell into Merck's structure.

Total addressable (all 10): ~$915K-1,395K Realistic conversion (top 7): ~$675-965K — which covers the $766K gap if weighted pipeline converts at ~50%.

3. Competitive Context

Merck is in defensive mode. The $3B cost-cutting and 6,000 job cuts signal MR budget pressure across all brands. Keytruda remains the franchise — over $25B in annual revenue — but the Keytruda cliff (LOE 2028-2030) is driving both cost discipline and aggressive pipeline investment (EyeBio, PCSK-9). Insights budgets are being scrutinized by procurement. ZoomRx's advantage: existing relationships through Rhonda Harp and proven delivery on Keytruda/HIV. Competitors (Spherix, InCrowd, DRG) will be aggressive on price. The restructuring into Merck Oncology vs. Merck Specialty creates two buying centers — this is both a risk (budget fragmentation) and opportunity (two separate budgets to sell into). AI adoption is stalled; the MSA needs an AI addendum before any AI-enabled offerings can be sold.

4. Connection to AO Plan / Gap Analysis

  • $766K gap is the central problem. Weighted pipeline at $671K nearly covers it, but pipeline-to-close conversion is never 100%.
  • Dave and Melissa own the AO relationship. Vinoth's role is execution excellence through East 7 and strategic support on expansion pitches.
  • The 20% growth target is ambitious given Merck's cost-cutting. Realistic path:
    • Renewals (items 1-4): ~$415-575K — this is the floor.
    • Net new wins needed: ~$200-350K — Gardasil PET + Keytruda Message Testing expansion are the most likely.
  • Risk: If Merck's restructuring causes any active project cancellation or non-renewal, the gap widens fast.
  • AI addendum in MSA is a blocker for the next wave of growth. This needs to be Dave/Melissa's priority with procurement (Jenn Ferrara-equivalent at Merck).

5. Recommended Next Actions for Vinoth

  1. Ensure East 7 delivery is flawless on all active Merck projects. Chanakya, Vidhya, and Madhur's work quality is Vinoth's most important BD contribution. Any delivery stumble at a $2.1M account during cost-cutting is an existential risk.
  2. Brief Dave/Melissa on Keytruda expansion angles. Prepare a one-pager on adding HNSCC or melanoma to PET. Make it easy for Melissa to pitch in her next Rhonda Harp conversation.
  3. Flag the AI addendum blocker. If Dave/Melissa haven't prioritized the MSA AI addendum, escalate. Without it, ZoomRx can't differentiate on the one capability that justifies premium pricing.
  4. Ask Rhonda Harp about EyeBio and PCSK-9 timelines. Even if these are speculative, getting on the radar early for launch services is how ZoomRx wins new brand entry. This is a Melissa ask, but Vinoth can prep the talking points.
  5. Monitor Gardasil PET reactivation closely. If this is genuinely being revisited, it could be $80-120K of fast revenue. Get status from Melissa.
  6. Do NOT spread East 7 thin. With $2.1M target and 6+ active projects, capacity management matters. If a new win comes in, Vinoth needs to have staffing ready — not scrambling.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
